text: Self-talk interventions have a positive moderate effect on sport task performance.
Self-talk interventions are more effective for tasks involving fine motor demands compared to gross motor demands.
Self-talk interventions are more effective for novel tasks compared to well-learned tasks.
Instructional self-talk is more effective than motivational self-talk for fine motor tasks.
Instructional self-talk is more effective for fine motor tasks than for gross motor tasks.
Self-talk interventions that include structured self-talk training are more effective than those without training.
